title: Bishop Pinned, Bishop Lost
setup: A black bishop sits on **d7**, directly in front of its king and directly in the rook's crosshairs.
hint: Pinned minor pieces are fixed targets — capture them.
explanation:
## The Idea

The black bishop on d7 is in an **absolute pin** along the d-file. Bishops can normally slither away on diagonals, but this one cannot move at all — any bishop move would put the king on d8 in check from the rook on d2.

White plays {{Rxd7+}}, snatching the bishop.

- {{Kxd7}} is forced — the king is the only piece that can respond to the check.
- After the trade, White has exchanged rook for bishop. In a fuller position this would be a **clean piece win**; in this miniature it is a teaching moment about the geometry.

## Pattern to Memorize

> The pin doesn't care what kind of piece it traps. Bishops, knights, rooks, queens — all become statues when pinned to their king.

The magic of a pin is that it **cancels the target's mobility entirely**, no matter how the piece normally moves.
